---
title: "Daymet"
format: html
---

# Setup  
```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

#install.packages("sf")
#install.packages("daymetr")
#install.packages("remotes")
#remotes::install_github("ropensci/USAboundaries")
#remotes::install_github("ropensci/USAboundariesData")

library(tidyverse)
library(USAboundaries) # for US state boundaries
library(sf) # for US map
library(daymetr)
```

```{r}
train_meta <- read_csv("../data/training/training_meta.csv")

train_meta
```

```{r}
daymet_all <- train_meta %>%
  mutate(
    weather = pmap(
      list(year, site, latitude, longitude),
      function(.y, .site, .lat, .lon) {

        # 🚫 skip invalid coords BEFORE API call
        if (is.na(.lat) || is.na(.lon) ||
            .lat < 14 || .lat > 83 ||
            .lon < -180 || .lon > -50) {
          return(NULL)
        }

        # 🛡️ safe download
        tryCatch({
          daymetr::download_daymet(
            site = paste0(.site, "_", .y),
            lat = .lat,
            lon = .lon,
            start = .y,
            end = .y,
            internal = TRUE,
            silent = TRUE
          )$data %>%
            as_tibble() %>%
            mutate(site = .site, year = .y)

        }, error = function(e) NULL)
      }
    )
  )

beepr::beep()

daymet_all %>% (head)

daymet_all$weather[1]

```


Now let's unnest the weather column.  
```{r}
daymet_all %>% 
  unnest(weather) #%>%
  

pivot_wider(names_from = measurement,
              values_from = value
              ) %>%
    janitor::clean_names()

```
Now, unnest the weather variable
```{r}
daymet_all_unnest <- daymet_all_unnes %>% 
  unnest(dayl_s:vp_pa)

daymet_all_unnest
```




# Exporting  
  
```{r}
write_csv(daymet_all_unnest,
          "../data/fieldd12weatherdata.csv"
          )
```
